header.author: No-Intro
header.trademarks: Product names used in this file are registered trademarks of their respective owners, with which No-Intro is in no way associated or affiliated. These trademarks of other manufacturers are used solely to identify said products.
header.piracy: Piracy harms consumers as well as legitimate developers, publishers and retailers. No-Intro does not help or encourage users to download or otherwise obtain any of the listed dumps. No-Intro is not in the Console Scene and is not involved in any P2P sharing.

archive.name: 007 - The World Is Not Enough
archive.sticky_note: Pressing Up while hovering over the "Start Game" option opens a cheats menu. Marked as NTSC, but has British English words and a language selection as in the European retail version.

archive.name: 007 - The World Is Not Enough
archive.sticky_note: "v21" prototype.

details.tool: GameShark Pro 3.2 and a old Compaq XP PC with a LPT-5 port along with the official GS software

archive.name: 007 - The World Is Not Enough
archive.sticky_note: "v2" is from "VER. 2.00" string in ROM

details.comment1: Hacked to make the game run better
details.comment2: [Released by: Forest of Illusion]
Cart label indicates a 12-15-98 date, but the ROM ucode indicates a later date "RSP Gfx ucode F3DEX fifo 2.08 Yoshitaka Yasumoto 1999 Nintendo".

archive.name: 1080 Snowboarding
archive.name_alt: テン・エイティ スノーボーディング

details.tool: ddrescue + custom recovery and decryption tools

archive.name: 3D Model RGB Test Program
archive.sticky_note: NTSC, but not clear if used a USA or European dev studio.

details.comment2: Released as BigEndian, not sure if dump tool produces that or not. Not sure if the version number on the label is accurate.

archive.name: 40 Winks
archive.sticky_note: Similar to this https://datomatic.no-intro.org/index.php?page=show_record&s=24&n=1076 / 40 Winks (USA) (Proto) / CRC32 3a674026, but unlike that, the ROM was modified by Piko Interactive.

details.origin: StoneAgeGamer Everdrive 64, official promotion with Piko Interactive
details.comment1: Files from the Everdrive SD card bought from StoneAgeGamer https://stoneagegamer.com/everdrive64-x7-base.html
Origin: Kickstarter backer cart from Piko
Source: Stone Age Gamer Everdrive bundle ROM download (NTSC)

archive.name: 40 Winks
archive.sticky_note: Similar to this https://datomatic.no-intro.org/index.php?page=show_record&s=24&n=1066 / 40 Winks (USA) (Aftermarket) (Unl) / CRC32 31c19633, but unlike that, the ROM wasn't modified by Piko Interactive.

details.tool: Retro Blaster v2.0c + firmware v2.8 + software v2.1.4
details.comment1: Marked as other as its not the original source of the proto ROM (i.e. proto cart, digital developer file, source code)

archive.name: 40 Winks
archive.sticky_note: Similar to this https://datomatic.no-intro.org/index.php?page=show_record&s=24&n=0008 / 40 Winks (Europe) (En,Es,It) (Proto) (1999-10-07) / CRC32 7e718143, but unlike that, the ROM was modified by Piko Interactive.

details.comment2: Barcode reading: 0745640946577

archive.name: 40 Winks
archive.sticky_note: Requires this patch to be used to get past a "This game can not be played on this system” screen, at least on some emulators/systems: https://www.romhacking.net/hacks/539/

details.origin: StoneAgeGamer Everdrive 64, official promotion with Piko Interactive
Source: Stone Age Gamer Everdrive bundle ROM download (PAL)

archive.name: AeroGauge
archive.sticky_note: Labelled as a kiosk demo in GoodTools, but may just be revision 0 (albeit possibly only found in carts with a demo label).

game.name: Belushi Speed Ball - What, Us Worry (World) (Audio) (Aftermarket) (Unl)

archive.name: Belushi Speed Ball - What, Us Worry

details.comment1: Originally overdumped. Prototype cart #20. Almost the same as CRC32 1AC60460 except for the bytes from 0x01000001 to 0x010CEF1F.
details.comment2:
Probably dumped by GreatGonzales [Comment from release: "thanks to @GreatGonzales"].
Comment by Zoinkity on archive.org item for this proto: This particular ROM is a build against debug libs before the final retail build of the PAL release. All files match retail PAL.

details.comment1: Originally overdumped. Prototype cart #53. Almost the same as CRC32 BB1CA04D except for the bytes from 0x01000001 to 0x010CEF1F.
details.comment2: Probably dumped by GamingLegend64 [Comment from release: "Many thanks to @GamingLegend64 for making this possible."]

details.comment: Underdump according to Nintendo lotcheck sheet

game.name: Bomberman Hero - Mirian Oujo o Sukue! (Japan)

archive.name: Bomberman Hero - Mirian Oujo o Sukue!
Bust-A-Move 3 DX
archive.sticky_note: Retail title is Bust-A-Move '99. Build date found in ROM.

details.comment2: Probably dumped by Baker64 [Link 3: https://n64prototypes.com/articles/Carnivale.htm] [Comment from release: "Massive thank you to Baker64 for sending this in!"]

details.tool: Interact Gameshark 3.3 via parallel port + Game Software Code Creator 2002

details.comment1: Verified according to GoodN64 V3.27 Missing bytes at 0x162390

game.name: Controller Kensa-ki NUS-yo N Cassette (Japan) (Test Program)

archive.name: Controller Kensa-ki NUS-yo N Cassette
archive.name_alt: コントローラ検査器 NUS用Nカセット
archive.sticky_note: Not clear if this was actually used for testing controllers or if this is just a prototype of the test program.
archive.datter_note: [Languages not checked][Title may not be romanised properly]

details.comment2: Cart label says "Ver. 00" on it. Physical media front image, dump date confirmation and dump tool info received privately from dumper. Released as BigEndian, not sure if dump tool produces that or not.

details.tool: Sanni Cartreader Hardware v3 running v10.2

details.comment1: Created at the same day as the final retail version, but has debug functionality.
details.comment2: Requires EEPROM 16K save type to boot.

details.comment2: ROM is corrupted as according to Zoinkity https://www.romhacking.net/hacks/6236/

archive.name: Destruction Derby 64
archive.sticky_note: Build date found in ROM

details.comment2: Control scheme sheet ID: NUS-NDYE-USA Manual Front ID: NUS-NDYE-USA

archive.name: Dinosaur Planet
archive.sticky_note: To bypass the Rare dev-ROM copy protection, you can replace 0xD0F6A7417D57761E at 0x10 with 0x906C3F77CE495EA1 (in the big endian ROM). SHA256 of cracked ROM: F8CAE52C3DF720604D89056F313B9BD77A6C3822191C7A8C59CD3A8C9B3A05F4

details.comment2: Note: Some text on the photo of the CD-ROM is censored File on CD-ROM was named "rom" and was dated 2020-12-01

archive.name: Dolphin Controller Test
archive.name_alt: ドルフィン・コントローラ・テスト
archive.sticky_note: Appears to be a program for testing early Dolphin controller prototypes.
archive.datter_note: todo: add rest of info - e.g. add photos, source url. see discussion on Rare Gaming Dump discord.

details.comment2: Cart label has the Nintendo 64 logo and says "開発用カートリッジ" (note: machine OCR, may not be 100% accurate), which translates to "Development Cartridge". The same label is seen on development cartridges from Spaceworld 95.

details.tool: RetroStage RetroBlaster v2.0c + firmware v2.5 + PC software v2.1.3b
Sanni cart reader v3 w/ firmware v8.4

details.comment: Underdump according to Nintendo lotcheck sheet and photo of PCB found online: https://www.tapatalk.com/groups/nintendo_64_forever/n64-cartridge-board-scan-picture-repository-comple-t2750.html https://farm1.staticflickr.com/771/31698491731_e525b81a92_h.jpg

details.comment1: Underdump according to Nintendo lotcheck sheet and ROM chip serial for 2022-04-05 dump
Full size dump - just some more FF padding on the end.
details.comment2: Same cart as PsyKo's 2022-04-05 dump.

details.tool: Mr. Backup Z64 - HW Rev 2 - BIOS Rev. 2.20b - HD modded

details.comment1: ROM was called "Undop0.559". ROM status set to Other to exclude this from verification, as this isn't from a cart.

details.tool: Mr. Backup Z64 - HW Rev 2 - BIOS Rev. 2.20b - HD modded

details.comment1: From USA Wii U Virtual Console Filename was "Undoe0.556". ROM status set to Other to exclude this from verification, as this isn't from a cart.

details.comment2: Manual Front ID: U/NUS-NDOE-USA

details.tool: ddrescue + custom recovery and decryption tools

file.note: Converted to BigEndian (.z64) from .n64 with ucon64

Converted to ByteSwapped (.v64) from the BigEndian (.z64) file hashes above with tool64 v1.11 (required adding padding for it to find the rom then removing padding after conversion) and ucon64 2.2.2. Both coversions matched these hashes.
Converted to BigEndian (.z64) from .n64 with ucon64

Converted to ByteSwapped (.v64) from the BigEndian (.z64) file hashes above with tool64 v1.11 (required adding padding for it to find the rom then removing padding after conversion) and ucon64 2.2.2. Both coversions matched these hashes.

archive.name: Doom 64
archive.sticky_note: Original BBS scene release SC-PACK1 is still MIA. Let us know if you own some info about it.

game.name: Doraemon 2 - Nobita to Hikari no Shinden (Japan)

archive.name: Doraemon 2 - Nobita to Hikari no Shinden

details.comment2:
[Link 4: https://www.nesworld.com/article.php?system=n64&data=n64-doubutsubanchou]
Cart was/is owned by NESWORLD
nfo file in release (which is styled as a scene release by the group CARROT) says "YECA" is the "supplier", which is "ACEY" backwards, aka NESWORLD. So he is probably the dumper.

details.comment1: Edition: Controller Pak Bundle Edition Original: コントローラーパック同梱版
details.comment2: Manual ID Front: NUS-NAFJ-JPN-2 Manual ID Back: V772778 Controller Pak ID: NUS-004 Controller Pak ID2: G744857 Controller Pak ID3: NUS-A-PA Controls insert ID: NUS-NAFJ-JPN V934733 Controller Pak PCB Marking: NUS-PA(K)-01 Controller Pak U1: Texas Instruments LV2416 2AM D69C [Battery Management Chip] Controller Pak U2: Winbond W24L257S70LL 108AB20447390-II1AC [Save Chip] Controller Pak BT1: CR2032 coin cell battery 02-09 (September 2002)

details.tool: ddrescue + custom recovery and decryption tools

archive.name: Dragon Sword 64
archive.sticky_note: Earlier Prototype version

details.comment1: Expanded to it's original size 32 MBit (filled with FF)

archive.name: Dragon Sword 64
archive.sticky_note: Later Prototype version

details.comment1: "The ROM is 11.3 MB in size. It was originally on a 32 MB dev cart but 20.7 MB was left blank so we resized it"
Expanded to it's original size 32 MBit (filled with FF)
Released as overdumped version of final USA release.

details.comment2: [Comment from release: "thanks to @GreatGonzales"]

details.comment1: [No hash confirmation] (hash mentioned seems to be header hash only?)

archive.name: ECW Hardcore Revolution
archive.sticky_note: Unfortunately it seem to be broken, thus this build is unbootable.

details.comment1: bad dump due to UltraSave making bad dumps for some proto boards

details.comment2: Updated upload on the site - redump using RetroBlaster

details.tool: ddrescue + custom recovery and decryption tools
ddrescue + custom recovery and decryption tools
Sanni's Open Source Cart Reader HW5 Rev3 + Software v9.1

archive.name: F-Zero X
archive.name_alt: エフゼロ エックス

game.name: F-Zero X (USA) (Beta) (The Legend of Zelda - Ocarina of Time leftover data)

archive.name: F-Zero X
archive.additional: The Legend of Zelda - Ocarina of Time leftover data
archive.sticky_note: First part of ROM (0x0-FFFFFF) contains final version of F-Zero X. Otherwise unused portion of ROM (0x1000000-1FFFFFF) contains about half of a late-1997 pre-final version of The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time (mostly just text, level and texture data).

details.tool: ddrescue + custom recovery and decryption tools

details.comment2: Voodooween probably dumped it [Comment from release: "Massive thanks to Voodooween for sending this in for release!"]

details.comment1: Gradiente release - no serial on outside. USA-ROM-chip on PCB

archive.name: Frogger 2
archive.sticky_note: Revision number in ROM header: 1

details.comment2: 256MBit ROM according to cart label

details.comment: Full cart dump, "overdumped" compared to ROM build size

archive.name: Frogger 2
archive.sticky_note: 1999-06-xx version.

details.comment2: Picture from https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mSuvh0pWwjE

details.comment: Full cart dump, "overdumped" compared to ROM build size

archive.name: Frogger 2
archive.sticky_note: 1999-06-xx version

details.comment2: Full cart dump, "overdumped" compared to ROM build size

archive.name: Frogger 2
archive.sticky_note: 1999-12-01 version. This ROM is from close to the end of development.

details.comment2: Dumped several times. Each dump was different beyond 12MiB due to some bad ROM chips not affecting the build. The build is byte-matched to the source code. Hashes are for the released ROM which is zeroed beyond 12MiB.

details.comment1: Only pictures were shared. SHA-1 (BigEndian) was found in Nekobabu's N64 ROM rename tool.

details.tool: ddrescue + custom recovery and decryption tools

game.name: Getter Love!! - Cho Renai Party Game Tanjou (Japan)

archive.name: Getter Love!! - Cho Renai Party Game Tanjou
Glover
archive.sticky_note: Limited Run Games release. Game copyright: Piko Interactive.

details.origin: StoneAgeGamer Everdrive 64, official promotion with Piko Interactive

details.comment1: ROM is corrupt, as found by Zoinkity http://www.romhacking.net/hacks/6219/
Checksum fixed, but this is not how the original ROM was, so status is set to "other"

details.comment2: Smudged text on back of cart seems to say "4th SEPT BUILD 35", although only the "SEPT" part is very clear.

details.comment1: Originally overdumped (24 MiB - lots of FF bytes on the end).
details.comment2:
Redump of same cart as CARROT release.
Unconfirmed dump date is from ROM file last modified date in release zip and from "redumped in 2020" line in archive.org page description,
Dumper identify and the fact that this is a redump of CARROT release cart privately confirmed to me (Hiccup) by dumper/uploader (NESWorld).

details.comment1: Trimmed CRC32 6dec2351 to 8 MiB, as after that its was just FFs (and in the original dump, past 24 MiB is garbage data). 8 MiB was the size of Glover 1 too.

details.comment: Dumper: NESWORLD/Acey/Martin Dump tool: GameShark Note: "YECA", is the name of the supplier in the NFO, which is ACEY backwards Dumper identity and dump tool privately confirmed to Hiccup by dumper. ROM has small corruptions in two places and has garbage data in overdump somewhere past the 24 MiB mark.

details.comment1: Trimmed scene release (CRC32 d0809afb) to 8 MiB.
Dumped twice with Retrode and sanni's Cart Dumper.

details.tool: Mr. Backup Z64 - HW Rev 2 - BIOS Rev. 2.20b - HD modded

archive.name: GoldenEye 007
archive.sticky_note: Modified USA ROM, apparently

details.comment2: Original filename: N-4631_e.z64
Date assumed from last modified date of file. NTSC boot chip CIC-NUS-6102. US file serial NGCE.

details.comment1: Trimmed to 96 Mbit (original retail release size).
The game does not have sounds or music yet.

details.comment2: USA region based on no language options.
USA region based on no language options

VRU Back ID: NUS-020(USA) VRU Back ID2: NUS-A-VRU-USA VRU Back Toolstamp code: 22 -VRU PCB- Serial: NUS-VRU(USA)-01 U1: Nintendo EVR-NUS 012 0033LG039 U2: NEC Japan D9930G 0030LY001 U3: Nintendo VC1-NUS 0027XY010 011

details.comment1: The good cart on the left at the picture
Not verified according to GoodN64 V3.27

details.comment1: Region part of serial hacked to say "E", even though it is actually a European ROM (clearly from the languages, frame rate etc).

details.comment1: Set region code in serial to "P".

archive.name: Nintendo 64 - PIF

details.comment1: Apparently includes PIF-RAM region (64 bytes of data at the end).
Removed the 64 bytes from the end.

details.tool: pif rom dumper and Everdrive x7

archive.name: Nintendo 64 - PIF

details.comment1: Apparently includes PIF-RAM region (64 bytes of data at the end).
Removed the 64 bytes from the end.

details.tool: pif rom dumper and Everdrive x7

archive.name: Nintendo 64 Modem
archive.sticky_note: Was used in Japan to allow internet connectivity through Randnet, in addition to the service's members-only portal sites.

details.comment2: Not a standard cart - cannot be dumped through normal means - dumping the ROM chip directly is apparently needed.

details.comment1:
Was originally submitted as an overdump:
BigEndian Size: 67108864 CRC32: 85B3AB37 MD5: A9BE6A493A680642D840F859A65816CA SHA-1: 2111D39265A317414D359E35A7D971C4DFA5F9E1
ByteSwapped Size: 67108864 CRC32: 7CAB0055 MD5: 24EDF142D2974627C218D2D507190C3B SHA-1: 7F29B70C9CB686AE678BEB40B59EE5583B095FF7

Was originally submitted as an overdump:
BigEndian Size: 67108864 CRC32: 85B3AB37 MD5: A9BE6A493A680642D840F859A65816CA SHA-1: 2111D39265A317414D359E35A7D971C4DFA5F9E1
ByteSwapped Size: 67108864 CRC32: 7CAB0055 MD5: 24EDF142D2974627C218D2D507190C3B SHA-1: 7F29B70C9CB686AE678BEB40B59EE5583B095FF7

archive.name: Pokemon Stadium
archive.sticky_note: This release is a different game than the "Pokemon Stadium" in other regions. The lack of P/C connection is intentional.

details.comment2: Manual Front ID: NUS-CPSJ-JPN Manual Back ID: G996739 Control scheme insert ID: NUS-CPSJ-JPN G509995 Box Flap ID: NUS-CPSJ-JPN V560462

details.comment1: GoodN64 V3.27 unreleased dump, just hashes provided

archive.name: Pokemon Stadium 2
archive.sticky_note: This game was released in other regions as "Pokemon Stadium". It is not the same game as other regions' "Pokemon Stadium 2".

details.comment1: Originally overdumped (full cart dump with leftover data).

serials.mediastamp: RAKUGAKIDS, Ver.0.1, CSソフト海外事業部, 1998/09/16 KCE神戸 No. 16, PAL板

details.comment1: This dump includes leftover data past the end of the game data

serials.media_serial2: RZ-018-E 1, RAKUGAKIDS, PAL用, Ver.1998/06/19, CSソフト海外事業部, 1998/06/19 KCE神戸 No. 1
serials.media_serial3: PAL用 (front), KCE神戸 開発部 005 (back)

details.comment1: Trimmed ROM file to it's original size. Leftover data is not a part of game data, doesn't matter if this is just a bulk of zeroes ("00 00 00 00 ...") or any other data (even thou a some part of another game).

details.comment: Underdump according to Nintendo lotcheck sheet

details.tool: ddrescue + custom recovery and decryption tools

details.tool: ddrescue + custom recovery and decryption tools
sanni's cartreader v3 with firmware v7.4

archive.name: Riqa
archive.sticky_note: Requires two controllers. Controller 2 is used to open the menu and select a level.

details.comment2: Original filename: riqa-a_14_02_2000.n64

archive.name: Riqa
archive.sticky_note: Requires two controllers. Controller 2 is used to open the menu and select a level.

details.comment2: Original filename: riqafinal1_24_03_2000.n64

archive.name: Riqa
archive.sticky_note:
Requires two controllers. Controller 2 is used to open the menu and select a level.
Included is a fix by the original developer as this build is malformed and doesn't work as-is.
This is the final build of Riqa according to the original developer, so it's assigned as the parent.
The original riqafinal1_28_05_2000.n64 proto is ByteSwapped by default, while all other protos are in the collection are BigEndian by default, so its hashes have been switched.

details.comment2: Original filenames: riqafinal1_28_05_2000.n64, riqafinal1_28_05_2000_fix.n64

archive.name: Riqa
archive.sticky_note: Requires two controllers. Controller 2 is used to open the menu and select a level.

details.comment2: Original filenames: riqafinal2_27_03_2000 [13_27].n64, riqafinal2_27_03_2000 [14_27].n64
Dumped multiple times with same result. Got dump tool info, number of dumps info and photos privately from dumper.

details.comment1: The dump by Anonymous, ehw and iniche was trimmed to cart capacity 256Mbit.

game.name: Robot Poncots 64 - 7tsu no Umi no Caramel (Japan)

archive.name: Robot Poncots 64 - 7tsu no Umi no Caramel

details.comment1: Date assumed from last modified date of file. NTSC boot chip CIC-NUS-6102. American developer GameTek.

archive.name: Rocket - Robot on Wheels
archive.sticky_note:
ROM contains the following version strings. I added backticks to mark the start + end of each string. ``beatbox`` ``09/29/99 17:23 ``0929.1723``
In game, they are displayed like this: ``0929.1723 beatbox`` Its not clear if the 2nd string is used. But anyway, it seems like the build date was 1999-09-29 and the buildtime was 17:23 (unk timezone).

details.comment2:
Probably dumped by AndrewEarley7 [Released by: Forest of Illusion] [Comment from release: "All thanks to the great @AndrewEarley7!"]
Dev cart has a USA CIC chip (and therefore USA region lock), despite the European ROM data.
by CARROT (former?) scene group?

details.comment1: dumped by Olivieryuyu and BigFred from the same cart

details.comment1: PAL prototype with a USA serial NBCE. Some emulators need the serial changed to a PAL one in order to run the ROM.

serials.mediastamp: SMAN, PROPERTY OF IGUANA ENTERTAINMENT LTD, 256meg Flash Rom Cartridge

details.comment1: Fixed to run in emulators (NBCE -> NBCU).

details.comment2: Seems to be a kiosk demo - front of box has a sticker on saying "この商品はサンプル品につき、|バックアップ機能はついておりません。". The PCB indeed does not have a save chip on it.

archive.name: Super Mario 64
archive.sticky_note: Revision number of 3 is used to mark this as the Shindou edition, rather than it using a different serial.

details.comment2: From Super Mario 3D All-Stars. Filename: 01_UNSMJ3.002.bin ROM status set to Other to exclude this from verification, as this isn't from a cart.

ddrescue + custom recovery and decryption tools

archive.name: Superman - The New Superman Aventures
archive.sticky_note: The title of this game according to the box is "Superman - The New Superman Aventures". This is the correct title. The actual box title is misspelled in the same way. Don't change it.

game.name: Tom and Jerry in Fists of Furry (USA)

archive.name: Tom and Jerry in Fists of Furry

details.comment2:
[Comment from release: "Massive thanks to @AndrewEarley7 for allowing us to release these"]
Video showed that its Hard4Games who dumped it
Front labels transcriptions (text that was censored in the photo is marked as "[censored]" and unclear parts are marked as "[?]"): ``` NUS-16F32SB 256M+256K ```
``` PROPERTY OF ACTIVISION INC. 010245 ```
``` ACTIVISION RETURN TO MOLLY HINCHEY [censored] [?] [?] [?] ```
Back label transcription: ``` TONY HAWK ```

archive.name: Tony Hawk's Pro Skater 2
archive.sticky_note: Marked as "Alpha" build by the developer

details.comment2:
[Comment from release: "Massive thanks to @AndrewEarley7 for allowing us to release these"]
Video showed that its Hard4Games who dumped it
Cart front labels transcriptions (html "strike" tag represents is used for strikethrough text and unclear parts are marked as "[?]"): ``` ACTIVISION THPS2 - <strike>MS3 03/01/01</strike> <strike>03/12/01</strike> ALPHA 4/20/01
3100 Ocean Park Boulevard Santa Monica, CA 9045 ```
``` [?] Los Angelos, California 90025 Telephone 310-473-9200 Facsimile 310-479-4005 ```

archive.name: Tony Hawk's Pro Skater 2
archive.sticky_note: v012 (Beta 2) version.

details.comment2:
[Comment from release: "Massive thanks to @AndrewEarley7 for allowing us to release these"]
Video showed that its Hard4Games who dumped it
Transcriptions of cart front labels (html "strike" tags are used for strikethrough text): ``` NUS-16F32SB-B 256M+256K ```
``` THPS2 n64 <strike>006</strike> <strike>BETA -052901</strike> <strike>BETA 2 06/04/01</strike> 009 010 011 012 ```
Transcription of cart back label: ``` ACTIVISION 3100 Ocean Park Boulevard Santa Monica, CA 90405 [censored]
Chris Hewish Producer ```

details.comment2: Info from video Bought by Night Dive Studios CEO Stephen Kick and lent to dumper to dump. Seller said he was a video game journalist that had received the cart from an ex-Aclaim employee

details.comment2: Dumped multiple times with same result. Got dump tool info, number of dumps info and photos privately from dumper.
